introSection: Iron Rattler is a Rocky Mountain Construction hybrid roller coaster located at Six Flags Fiesta Texas in San Antonio, Texas, USA. The coaster spends half of its course on top of the cliffs surrounding the park, utilizing the Rattler's helix structure for the inversion and two over-banked curves. Iron Rattler interacts with Gully Washer, the train ride, and Road Runner Express.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
On August 30, 2012, Six Flags Fiesta Texas announced the addition of Iron Rattler. Iron Rattler opened on May 25, 2013.

In July 2013, Iron Rattler temporarily closed after a woman was killed on New Texas Giant at Six Flags Over Texas. The attraction eventually reopened the following month with new seatbelts.

sections.title: Ride experience
sections.content: As the train moves out of the station, it makes a left turn. Riders then head up a tall chain lift hill. At the top, riders are being held for a few seconds before dropping feet at an 81° angle, reaching a max speed of. The train ascends up a cliff, passing through a 110° over-banked curve and a 95° over-banked curve. Soon afterwards, the train makes a left-handed dive off the cliff and goes straight into a  tall zero-g roll. Riders move through two more over-banked curves before plunging 100 feet off the cliff. The train turns left into a tunnel before returning to the station.

fullText:
Iron Rattler is a Rocky Mountain Construction hybrid roller coaster located at Six Flags Fiesta Texas in San Antonio, Texas, USA. The coaster spends half of its course on top of the cliffs surrounding the park, utilizing the Rattler's helix structure for the inversion and two over-banked curves. Iron Rattler interacts with Gully Washer, the train ride, and Road Runner Express.

On August 30, 2012, Six Flags Fiesta Texas announced the addition of Iron Rattler. Iron Rattler opened on May 25, 2013.

In July 2013, Iron Rattler temporarily closed after a woman was killed on New Texas Giant at Six Flags Over Texas. The attraction eventually reopened the following month with new seatbelts.



As the train moves out of the station, it makes a left turn. Riders then head up a tall chain lift hill. At the top, riders are being held for a few seconds before dropping feet at an 81° angle, reaching a max speed of. The train ascends up a cliff, passing through a 110° over-banked curve and a 95° over-banked curve. Soon afterwards, the train makes a left-handed dive off the cliff and goes straight into a  tall zero-g roll. Riders move through two more over-banked curves before plunging 100 feet off the cliff. The train turns left into a tunnel before returning to the station.
